docs.title: Onboarding Guide
docs.text: Welcome to the Rupin hardware team. This page gets you set up and oriented quickly.

docs.title: What is Rupin?
docs.text:
Rupin is a home automation product line. The hardware consists of PCBs that handle touch input, fan speed control, and WiFi-based control — all designed to work together as a system.

See the System Architecture for how the boards connect.

docs.title: Tools You Need
docs.text: Tool Purpose Download KiCad 8.x Open and edit PCB designs kicad.org Git Version control git-scm.com A web browser View iBOM files —

docs.title: Getting the Hardware Files
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Otomator-Technologies/rupin-hardware.git

Each product lives in its own folder, versioned by release:

rupin-hardware/
├── RP-TS1H16A/
│   ├── RP-TS1H16A v3.0.0/
│   └── RP-TS1H16A v3.0.1/    ← latest
├── RP-TS2S10A/
│   └── ...
└── ...

docs.title: Opening a Project in KiCad
docs.text:
1. Open KiCad
  2. File → Open Project
  3. Navigate to RP-XXXX/RP-XXXX vX.X.X/
  4. Select the .kicad_pro file

docs.title: Understanding the Output Files
docs.text:
All production-ready files are in Outputs/production/ inside each version folder:

File What it is *_GERBER.zip Send to PCB manufacturer for fabrication *_BOM.csv Bill of materials for component procurement *_iBOM.html Open in browser — interactive assembly reference *_POS.csv Pick & place file for SMT assembly *_SCH.pdf Schematic PDF for reference *.step 3D model for mechanical fit check

docs.title: Getting a Production Release
docs.text: Go to rupin-hardware releases and download the PRODUCT-VERSION-production.zip for the board you need. It contains everything the manufacturer requires.

    TS2["Touch Switch\nRP-TS2S10A"]:1
    FR["Fan Regulator\nRP-FR3SP"]:1

    App --> Controller
    Controller --> TS1
    Controller --> TS2
    Controller --> FR

docs.text: Describe the communication protocol between boards (e.g. UART, I2C, RS485, proprietary). Include baud rates, addressing scheme, packet format if applicable.

docs.text: Describe the board's role — the WiFi bridge between the mobile app / cloud and the load-side boards (touch switches, fan regulator). Controls 2 channels.

docs.text:

  • Load board interface — describe: how it communicates to TS / FR boards

docs.title: Calculations
docs.text: Engineering calculations referenced in the Rupin hardware designs.

docs.title: SMPS Output Voltage
docs.text:
Add resistor divider calculation for feedback voltage.

\[V_{out} = V_{ref} \times \left(1 + \frac{R_1}{R_2}\right)\]

Example: add component values and result here.

docs.title: LDO Dropout & Heat Dissipation
docs.text:
\[P_{dissipated} = (V_{in} - V_{out}) \times I_{load}\]

Add values per board.

docs.title: Sensitivity & CS Pin Capacitance
docs.text: Describe electrode area, trace length, parasitic capacitance, and TTP223 sensitivity adjustment.

docs.title: Base Resistor Calculation
docs.text:
\[R_B = \frac{V_{GPIO} - V_{BE}}{I_B} = \frac{V_{GPIO} - 0.7}{I_C / h_{FE}}\]

Add coil current, transistor hFE, and computed RB per board.

docs.text: Describe how capacitor values are selected for each fan speed step. Add calculation or reference.

docs.title: Design Review Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] DRC passes with zero errors
  • [ ] All footprints have 3D models
  • [ ] Version number on silkscreen
  • [ ] Power nets verified (correct polarity, no floating pins)
  • [ ] Mains isolation clearance checked
  • [ ] BOM exported and verified
  • [ ] Gerbers reviewed in Gerber viewer before release
